title: mfa — model relationships
description: WebauthnCredential is the ONLY row owned directly by this module — every other MFA state lives inline on the Identity row (identity/identity ownership) or in the shared auth_mfa_challenges ledger (identity/auth ownership). Consequently the FK topology here is intentionally shallow: WebauthnCredential belongs-to Identity, and Identity has-many WebauthnCredentials. The rich inbound / outbound relations chart lives on identity/identity — this file only documents mfa-owned rows.

internal.description: Every WebAuthn credential belongs to exactly one Identity. Cascade on delete so a hard-purged Identity (post-erasure-hold-period) drops every registered authenticator in the same transaction. Never orphan — a WebAuthn credential without an Identity is a compliance failure.

outbound.description: Same FK as the internal row above — repeated here as an outbound edge for the substrate-crossing view. Identity is a GLOBAL aggregate; WebauthnCredential is likewise global by transitivity (no tenant_id, no application_id). A hardware key registered by an Identity is usable across every Application that Identity is provisioned into.

inbound.description: Identity has-many WebauthnCredentials via `webauthn_credentials.identity_id`. No other model in the tenant references a WebauthnCredential row directly — challenges reference the credential by `credential_id` (the WebAuthn-native bytea handle) which we resolve at verification time, not by our internal ULID.

hasMany.description: Accessor: `$identity->webauthnCredentials` returns every registered credential (including disabled ones — filter with `->where('disabled_at', null)` for the active set).

shared_table_references.description: MFA challenges transit the shared `auth_mfa_challenges` table owned by identity/auth. This module reads + writes rows there via identity/auth's `MfaChallengeRepository` contract but never declares FKs on it, and never joins against it from Eloquent (challenges are ephemeral, 5-minute TTL, resolved by the challenge_id in the request payload).
referenced_columns: id (challenge ULID `mfc_<26>` — resolved by the /me/mfa/challenge/{challenge_id}/verify route)
identity_id (FK to identities.id — resolves the caller for constant-time secret lookup)
method (enum: totp / webauthn / recovery_code — dispatches to the right challenger)
attempts_remaining (decrement per failed verify; challenge invalidates on zero)

accessor_chain.description: WebauthnCredential exposes computed accessors that downstream code reads instead of touching the bytea columns directly.

WebauthnCredential.credential_id_b64url: base64url encoding of the `credential_id` bytea (used in the WebAuthn assertion payload the browser sends)
WebauthnCredential.public_key_cose: COSE-decoded public-key object (used by the assertion verifier)
WebauthnCredential.is_platform_authenticator: 'internal' IN transports (heuristic — platform authenticators typically report `internal`; roaming keys typically report `usb` / `nfc` / `ble` / `hybrid`)
WebauthnCredential.is_synced_passkey: backup_state = true (the credential is currently synchronised across the user's cloud accounts, e.g. iCloud Keychain / Google Password Manager)
